mechanism_narrative: The consumer price index at 233.887 represents elevated price level relative to pre-pandemic trend. Persistently high CPI engrains inflation expectations, causing investors to demand higher term premium on the 10-year Treasury, pushing yields up over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:CPI level well above pre-COVID trend
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year Rank IC between CPIAUCSL level and 20-day forward yield change falls below 0.03, or t-test of positive mean return after CPI above 230 fails at 5% significance.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 6.7% is falling from pandemic peak. A trigger of UNRATE crossing below 6.0 signals accelerating labor market tightness. Markets anticipate the Federal Reserve will need to raise rates to prevent overheating, lifting 10-year yields.
failure_condition: After the trigger fires, more than 60% of 20-day forward yield changes are negative over a 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: With the federal funds rate at 0.09% (effectively zero) and CPI at 233.887 (elevated), the real policy rate is deeply negative. Markets expect the Fed to eventually tighten to combat inflation. The joint presence of near-zero rates and high consumer prices intensifies rate hike expectations, pushing 10-year yields higher.
evidence_refs: fact:Real fed funds rate deeply negative
failure_condition: Joint signal produces a Sharpe ratio below 0.5 for 20-day yield changes over a 3-year walk-forward, or pairs trade fails to outperform a single-factor model.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP at 15839.3 billion is near pre-pandemic peak. In an expansion regime where GDP exceeds 16 trillion, the economy operates with positive output gap. Strong demand pressures fuel inflation and expected monetary tightening, causing 10-year yields to rise over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The sign of the predictive relationship between GDPC1 level and 20-day yield change is not opposite in expansions vs. recessions (IC spread < 0.05 over 3-year rolling periods).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts at 1091 thousand and industrial production at 101.28 are both historically moderate. When both series rise above their respective 3-month moving averages, it signals broad-based demand strength. This combination leads markets to price in tighter monetary policy, raising 10-year yields.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 3-month MA of HOUST and INDPRO > 3-month MA of INDPRO
failure_condition: Combined signal delivers information coefficient below 0.10 over a 2-year walk-forward, or the individual signals do not improve predictive accuracy when combined.

mechanism_narrative: Retail and food services sales at 431879 million dollars indicate robust consumer spending. When RSAFS exceeds 450000, it signals strong demand that may overheat the economy. Markets anticipate Fed tightening to cool consumption, driving the 10-year yield higher.
failure_condition: After trigger events, the proportion of negative 20-day yield changes exceeds 50% in a 2-year backtest, or the mean return is not statistically different from zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production at 101.28 is above its 2017 base. A persistently rising INDPRO level reflects solid industrial output that supports economic growth. This sustained momentum increases the risk of capacity constraints and higher yields, with the effect showing quickly within one week.
failure_condition: Rolling 1-year Rank IC of INDPRO level on 5-day forward yield changes falls below 0.02, or the strategy fails to produce positive average returns over a 5-year walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-unemployment regime (UNRATE > 6%), nonfarm payrolls changes carry different implications. Rising payrolls (PAYEMS) signal recovery that reduces recession risk, while falling payrolls disincline the Fed to tighten. Given current UNRATE 6.7%, a declining PAYEMS level would be interpreted as weakness, leading markets to expect easier monetary policy and lower yields.
failure_condition: In the designated regime, the directional sign of the PAYEMS signal on 20-day yield changes is not opposite to its sign in low-unemployment regimes (regime-specific IC spread < 0.05 over any 3-year window).
